title: Hematuria
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports history, physical examination, blood pressure, creatinine, gross-haematuria history, irritative urinary symptoms, smoking and occupational exposure, family history, anticoagulant context, urinary infection context, and flank-pain context.
examination.rationale: Supports mapped gross or microscopic history, urinary symptoms, anticoagulant, smoking, occupational, family, UTI, flank-pain, blood-pressure, creatinine, and combined-history fields.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports history, physical examination, blood pressure, creatinine, gross-haematuria history, irritative urinary symptoms, smoking and occupational exposure, family history, anticoagulant context, urinary infection context, and flank-pain context.

investigations.text: Supports clinician shared-decision follow-up and reassessment when gross haematuria or new urinary symptoms occur, without automatic investigation or referral output.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped recurrent gross-haematuria prompt, safety-net discussion, and clinician-arranged follow-up without automatic investigation or referral.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports clinician shared-decision follow-up and reassessment when gross haematuria or new urinary symptoms occur, without automatic investigation or referral output.

assessment.text: Supports urinalysis with microscopic confirmation and microhaematuria terminology without assigning cause.
assessment.rationale: Supports mapped microhaematuria, urinalysis, and microscopy terminology without assigning cause.
assessment.evidence_extract: Supports urinalysis with microscopic confirmation and microhaematuria terminology without assigning cause.

additions.text: Supports urinalysis with microscopic confirmation and microhaematuria terminology without assigning cause.

source_limitations: The broad workflow does not encode adult, microscopic, risk-stratum, post-infection, renal-disease, or prior-negative-evaluation qualifiers.

Urinary retention, fever, abdominal or flank examination, general vitals, clots, painless visible haematuria, weight loss, imaging, previous records, referral, generic clinician plan, fixed follow-up intervals, and PRN wording remain unsupported.
Cause, cancer risk, diagnosis, imaging, cystoscopy, referral, treatment, and exact follow-up timing remain clinician responsibilities.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
